item.text:
**Considering refactoring constraints**

I need to work on a repository, likely with Spring Boot, and the user wants me to refactor a feature into layers with validation and exception handling. I should probably use the spring-boot-engineer skill and check relevant documentation, but I also wonder if it's necessary. Given the read-only status of the sandbox, I can't actually modify files unless instructed. I'll consider providing a copy-pastable patch instead, since direct edits aren't possible. I'll need to inspect the repository to determine the feature and look for any AGENTS in subdirectories.

**Exploring patch options**

I’m wondering about the danger of full access, but the permission instructions clearly say it’s read-only. Since we can’t ask for approval, maybe trying to escalate won’t work either. I should rely on my skills and provide a fallback since I can’t modify files directly. I’m still contemplating using apply_patch, but that might not be feasible. The next step is to inspect the repository structure and propose a patch while keeping my plan intact. Let’s get started!
Using `spring-boot-engineer` here because this is a Spring Boot layering, validation, and exception-handling refactor.

item.command: bash -lc "printf 'CWD: '; pwd; echo; echo 'Root files:'; rg --files -g 'AGENTS.md' -g 'pom.xml' -g 'build.gradle*' -g 'settings.gradle*' -g 'src/**' . | sed 's#"'^./##'"' | head -n 400"
bash -lc "printf 'CWD: '; pwd; echo; echo 'Root files:'; rg --files -g 'AGENTS.md' -g 'pom.xml' -g 'build.gradle*' -g 'settings.gradle*' -g 'src/**' . | sed 's#"'^./##'"' | head -n 400"

item.text: First I’m locating the Spring Boot project and any nested instructions.

item.command: bash -lc "find .. -maxdepth 3 \\( -name AGENTS.md -o -name pom.xml -o -name build.gradle -o -name build.gradle.kts -o -path '*/src/main/java' -o -path '*/src/test/java' \\) | sed 's#"'^../##'"' | sort"

bash -lc "find .. -maxdepth 3 \\( -name AGENTS.md -o -name pom.xml -o -name build.gradle -o -name build.gradle.kts -o -path '*/src/main/java' -o -path '*/src/test/java' \\) | sed 's#"'^../##'"' | sort"
